<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
    <meta charset="UTF-8">
    <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, user-scalable=yes">
    <title>Coca-Cola · Website Proposal & Readme</title>
    <!-- No CSS included — pure semantic HTML with structural readability -->
</head>
<body>

    <!-- ========================================= -->
    <!-- README-STYLE PROPOSAL DOCUMENT             -->
    /* No CSS — only HTML structure, headings, lists, tables, and inline semantics */
    <!-- ========================================= -->

    <main>
        <!-- MAIN TITLE / ORGANISATION HEADER -->
        <header>
            <h1>🍹 Coca-Cola · Website Proposal</h1>
            <p><strong>Organisation view:</strong> Coca-Cola</p>
            <hr>
        </header>

        <!-- 1. ORGANISATION BACKGROUND -->
        <section>
            <h2>1. Organisation Overview</h2>
            <p><strong>Name:</strong> Coca-Cola</p>
            <p><strong>History:</strong> Coca-Cola was founded in 1886 and has grown into one of the world’s most iconic beverage companies — dedicated to refreshment and making a difference.</p>
            <p><strong>Mission:</strong> To refresh the world, make a difference.</p>
            <p><strong>Vision:</strong> To create brands people love.</p>
            <p><strong>Target audiences:</strong> Teenagers, young adults and families.</p>
        </section>

        <!-- 2. WEBSITE GOALS & OBJECTIVES (with KPIs) -->
        <section>
            <h2>2. Website Goals & Objectives</h2>
            <ul>
                <li>Promote Coca-Cola products effectively</li>
                <li>Increase brand awareness across digital channels</li>
                <li>Provide clear, accessible product information</li>
                <li>Increase online engagement (interactions, content exploration)</li>
            </ul>
            <p><strong>Key Performance Indicators (KPIs):</strong></p>
            <ul>
                <li>Website traffic (unique visitors & sessions)</li>
                <li>Time spent on site (average session duration)</li>
                <li>Product clicks / interaction rate on product pages</li>
            </ul>
        </section>

        <!-- 3. CURRENT WEBSITE ANALYSIS (Strengths, Weaknesses, Improvements) -->
        <section>
            <h2>3. Current Website Analysis</h2>
            <p><strong>Strengths:</strong> Strong branding, clear visuals that resonate with the Coca-Cola identity.</p>
            <p><strong>Weaknesses:</strong> Too much information on homepage → cognitive overload for visitors.</p>
            <p><strong>Improvements proposed:</strong> Simpler navigation structure and faster loading speed (image optimization, cleaner layout).</p>
        </section>

        <!-- 4. PROJECT SCOPE: PAGES, DURATION, COST ESTIMATE -->
        <section>
            <h2>4. Project Scope & Deliverables</h2>
            <p><strong>Core pages to be developed:</strong> Homepage, Products, and Contact.</p>
            <p><strong>Project duration:</strong> 4 Weeks (design, development, testing, deployment).</p>
            <p><strong>Estimated cost range:</strong> R700 – R1400 (South African Rand) depending on final feature set and revisions.</p>
        </section>

        <!-- 5. AUDIENCE & PURPOSE (Important Points) -->
        <section>
            <h2>5. Target Audience & Core Purpose</h2>
            <p><strong>Primary audience:</strong> Teens and Families.</p>
            <p><strong>Purpose of the website:</strong> To advertise the products of Coca-Cola, share brand stories, and drive engagement with the Coca-Cola universe.</p>
        </section>

        <!-- 6. STRUCTURE: HOMEPAGE, PRODUCTS, CONTENT HIGHLIGHTS -->
        <section>
            <h2>6. Proposed Page Structure & Content Strategy</h2>

            <h3>🏠 Homepage</h3>
            <p>Hero visual with iconic Coca-Cola imagery, clear call-to-action (Explore Products). Simplified navigation menu (Products, Contact). Optimised to reduce clutter — key highlights: refreshing campaigns, limited offers, brand heritage snapshot.</p>

            <h3>🥤 Products</h3>
            <p>Showcase Coca-Cola variants: Classic, Zero Sugar, Diet Coke, plus other brand extensions (Fanta, Sprite where applicable). Each product tile includes description, nutritional facts (optional), and engagement click (product clicks KPI). Filter by category / type to improve usability.</p>

            <h3>📞 Contact</h3>
            <p>Simple contact form (name, email, message) and customer support details. Also links to official Coca-Cola social channels and FAQ references. Fast-loading and minimal design.</p>
        </section>

        <!-- 7. KEY DELIVERABLES + TIMELINE (4 weeks) -->
        <section>
            <h2>7. Project Timeline (4 Weeks)</h2>
            <!-- using a simple table to present timeline clearly, no CSS, only HTML table -->
            <table border="1" cellpadding="6" cellspacing="0" style="border-collapse: collapse; width: 100%; max-width: 800px;">
                <thead>
                    <tr>
                        <th>Week</th>
                        <th>Activities</th>
                        <th>Deliverables</th>
                    </tr>
                </thead>
                <tbody>
                    <tr>
                        <td>Week 1</td>
                        <td>Wireframing, content audit, simplified navigation design</td>
                        <td>Low-fidelity homepage & products layout, navigation structure</td>
                    </tr>
                    <tr>
                        <td>Week 2</td>
                        <td>Visual design (brand-compliant), product page development</td>
                        <td>High-fidelity mockups + interactive prototype</td>
                    </tr>
                    <tr>
                        <td>Week 3</td>
                        <td>HTML/CSS (functional), contact form integration, speed optimization</td>
                        <td>Fully coded responsive pages (Home, Products, Contact)</td>
                    </tr>
                    <tr>
                        <td>Week 4</td>
                        <td>Testing, cross-browser checks, KPI tracking setup, deployment</td>
                        <td>Live proposal site + performance report</td>
                    </tr>
                </tbody>
            </table>
        </section>

        <!-- 8. SUCCESS METRICS (KPIs & TARGETS) -->
        <section>
            <h2>8. Success Metrics & KPI Targets</h2>
            <ul>
                <li><strong>Website traffic:</strong> +20% increase in unique visitors (month-over-month).</li>
                <li><strong>Time spent on site:</strong> Average session duration &gt; 2 minutes (improved engagement).</li>
                <li><strong>Product clicks:</strong> At least 35% of visitors click on at least one product tile.</li>
                <li><strong>Bounce rate reduction:</strong> Lower homepage bounce rate by simplifying information density.</li>
            </ul>
        </section>

        <!-- 9. REFERENCES (official and academic) -->
        <section>
            <h2>9. References & Resources</h2>
            <ul>
                <li>Official Coca-Cola website (coca-cola.com) — brand guidelines & visual identity reference.</li>
                <li>Marketing textbooks — consumer engagement strategies, brand storytelling.</li>
                <li>Web design resources (usability heuristics, accessibility best practices).</li>
                <li>Internal project documentation: audience insights (teens & families).</li>
            </ul>
        </section>

        <!-- 10. ADDITIONAL NOTES: BUDGET & NEXT STEPS -->
        <section>
            <h2>10. Budget Range & Next Steps</h2>
            <p><strong>Estimated Cost:</strong> R700 – R1400 (based on scope: 3 core pages, 4-week timeline, no backend complexities).</p>
            <p><strong>Next Steps:</strong> Final approval of design direction → begin Week 1 wireframes. After approval, full HTML implementation without external CSS frameworks (ensuring readability and minimal overhead).</p>
            <p><em>All deliverables will follow semantic HTML, accessibility principles, and performance-first approach to address loading speed weaknesses.</em></p>
        </section>

        <!-- 11. README summary block (proposal readme) -->
        <hr>
        <footer>
            <h2>📄 README · Coca-Cola Website Proposal</h2>
            <p><strong>Project name:</strong> Coca-Cola Brand Experience Website (Redesign/Refresh)</p>
            <p><strong>Purpose:</strong> This document serves as a complete README and proposal outline for the Coca-Cola promotional website. It defines the project's goals, audience (teens & families), core pages (Homepage, Products, Contact), duration (4 weeks), and cost estimate (R700–R1400). The primary mission is to advertise Coca-Cola products, increase brand awareness, provide product information, and boost online engagement — tracked via traffic, time-on-site, and product clicks.</p>
            <p><strong>Improvement focus:</strong> Simplify homepage navigation and improve loading speed while retaining Coca-Cola’s strong branding and clear visuals.</p>
            <p><strong>Technical note:</strong> The final website will be built using <strong>only HTML</strong> (no CSS, no external styling frameworks) as required, ensuring maximum readability, semantic structure, and baseline accessibility. All content will be structured using headings, lists, tables, and semantic elements to mirror a clean document-style proposal or readme.</p>
            <p><strong>Status:</strong> Draft proposal — pending approval. After approval, coded HTML pages (Home, Products, Contact) will be delivered within 4 weeks.</p>
            <p><em>— Coca-Cola digital team / agency proposal, aligned with brand values “refresh the world, make a difference”.</em></p>
        </footer>

        <!-- optional: simple divider to denote end of proposal -->
        <hr>
        <p><small>Coca-Cola Website Proposal · Readme format · No CSS used — compliant with request: "only html and code it for me".</small></p>
    </main>
</body>
</html>
